21:22.arabic: لَوْ كَانَ فِيهِمَآ ءَالِهَةٌ إِلَّا ٱللَّهُ لَفَسَدَتَا
21:22.english: Had there been gods in them (heavens and earth) other than Allah, they would have been ruined.
21:22.score_notes: Famous logical argument for tawhid — short and self-contained.

21:63.arabic: قَالَ بَلْ فَعَلَهُۥ كَبِيرُهُمْ هَـٰذَا فَسْـَٔلُوهُمْ إِن كَانُوا۟ يَنطِقُونَ
21:63.english: He said: Rather, their chief did it — ask them, if they can speak.
21:63.score_notes: Ibrahim's wit when questioned about smashing the idols — brilliant story.

lesson_use.heading: 10 · كَبِير (great, big)
lesson_use.arabic: قَالَ بَلْ فَعَلَهُ **كَبِيرُهُمْ** هَٰذَا فَاسْأَلُوهُمْ إِن كَانُوا يَنطِقُونَ
lesson_use.english: He said: Rather, their **chief** did it — ask them, if they can speak
lesson_use.notes: Ibrāhīm again — this time using wit. He smashed the idols and blamed the biggest one. Notice **كَبِيرُهُمْ** — "their biggest/chief." Same root, now describing a statue.

21:87.arabic: وَذَا ٱلنُّونِ إِذ ذَّهَبَ مُغَـٰضِبًا فَظَنَّ أَن لَّن نَّقْدِرَ عَلَيْهِ فَنَادَىٰ فِى ٱلظُّلُمَـٰتِ أَن لَّآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّآ أَنتَ سُبْحَـٰنَكَ إِنِّى كُنتُ مِنَ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ
21:87.english: He called out in the darkness: There is no god but You, glory be to You, I was among the wrongdoers.
21:87.score_notes: Story of Yunus in the whale — one of the most beloved du'as. Too long for beginner's first phrase but excellent for later lessons.

lesson_use.rejection_reason: Too long for a beginner's first phrase. Student found it overwhelming.
